spec_match.rationale: Concepts exercise the exact current WICG surface with the correct bindings throughout: the five instance methods called on crypto.subtle (encapsulateBits/decapsulateBits round-trip, ML-DSA sign/verify + getPublicKey, ChaCha20-Poly1305 encrypt/decrypt), and the static feature-detection method called exclusively as SubtleCrypto.supports(...) on the interface object — the support probe, ML-KEM and ChaCha pages all check typeof SubtleCrypto !== 'undefined' && typeof SubtleCrypto.supports === 'function' before calling. Verified against the unchanged WICG IDL (re-fetched 2026-07-24; two supports overloads) and Chromium main subtle_crypto.idl (all six new members RuntimeEnabled=WebCryptoPQC).

interactive.rationale: Real controls drive real crypto with honest negative branches: param-set selects, run buttons whose failures surface the real exception (never simulated success), tamper flows that must flip verification/decryption, a full support matrix whose cells report true/false/unknown distinctly, and a wrong-nonce OperationError probe.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Fixed the family index's binding error: the support-probe card said 'crypto.subtle.supports(...)' — corrected to SubtleCrypto.supports(...) on the interface object with an explicit not-the-instance note. Parent review also separated the five instance methods from the static in the lede and made the probe snippets safe when the SubtleCrypto global itself is absent by using globalThis.SubtleCrypto?.supports?.(...), rather than optional-chaining from a potentially undeclared identifier. Also renamed 'why it shipped' to 'why it's proposed' and added an exact availability statement (dev trial 150, registerable origin trial desktop 151–154 with origin_trial_id 1379790335835635713, no default-on milestone, status 'Proposed', chromestatus API updated 2026-06-17).

edge_cases.rationale: Honest negative branches everywhere: flag-off flag-notes and honest failures, the matrix's three-way cell semantics (true/false/unknown) with the flag-note now precise that cells read '—' (unknown) while the method itself is absent, X-Wing notably absent from the concept set (it is in the chromestatus summary but not implemented — the v150 capability-matrix documents that; this family probes only registered names).

references.rationale: Index cites chromestatus.com/feature/5198951632470016, the WICG modern-algos draft, and the FIPS/RFC normative references; the support probe links the spec's #SubtleCrypto-method-supports anchor (verified present); suite anchors verified against the spec's real IDs.

conformance.rationale: New immutable root suite (this cycle, 9 assertions): host existence, the five instance-method typeof checks on crypto.subtle, the static supports typeof on SubtleCrypto (interface object), supports-not-on-instance (anti-regression for the static-vs-instance trap, holds in any browser), and the [SecureContext] gate. No assertion performs crypto work — typeof/binding checks only, safe on any build. Executes live at /v151/algorithm-updates-in-webcrypto/conformance/.

runtime_evidence.rationale: chrome-devtools-mcp on desktop Chrome stable (2026-07-24): all visible controls exercised at 1280×800 and 390×844 — support probe (method absent → all '—' cells + honest status + corrected flag-note), ML-KEM run (real NotSupportedError reported, no fake success), ML-DSA sign (real failure surfaced), ChaCha encrypt (disabled or honest failure), zero console errors and zero failed requests. No crypto operations were auto-executed beyond safe typeof/binding probes.

telemetry_health.rationale: Concepts emit showcaseTelemetry.assert events per branch (supports-method-present, mlkem-roundtrip, mldsa sign/verify outcomes, chacha outcomes); telemetry POSTs returned 202 during validation.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables throughout, button background+color pairs, focus-visible outlines, matrix swatches paired with text labels (not color-only), no static-card pattern; this cycle's edits were copy-level and preserve layout.

accessibility.rationale: role=status/aria-live on status lines and single-probe output, labelled selects, matrix cells carry per-cell aria-labels, the legend explains swatch meanings in text.

mobile_desktop_parity.rationale: Matrix pass: all five pages fit at 1280×800 and 390×844; the matrix table sits in an overflow-x:auto wrapper. Parent review found the generated single-probe result widened the page to 407px only after the button was exercised, added overflow-wrap:anywhere to that result surface, then re-verified page scrollWidth equals the 390px viewport; buttons measure ≈45px.

openQuestions.title: Re-verify when the origin trial ends or a default-on milestone appears
openQuestions.detail: Copy is provenanced to the 2026-07-24 fetch of the direct chromestatus API (updated 2026-06-17: dev trial 150, OT desktop 151–154 with origin_trial_id 1379790335835635713, ship estimate unset, status 'Proposed') and Chromium main subtle_crypto.idl. When the OT ends (after Chrome 154), a ship estimate appears, or status moves off 'Proposed', update the index availability statement and re-run the suite.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live results matrix table with per-cell state labels
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce dynamic state through live regions; pair every color/state swatch with a text label; give table cells programmatic names.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Verified applied on the support probe: status line is role=status aria-live, matrix cells carry aria-labels ('ML-KEM-768 encapsulateBits: supported/not supported/not applicable or unknown'), and the legend is textual.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible form controls selects checkboxes and labeled inputs in interactive demo pages
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Associate labels with every input/select and expose state changes programmatically.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Verified applied: operation/algorithm and parameter-set selects are wrapped in label elements with visible text; state changes announce via role=status.

summary: v151-route review of the WebCrypto family (origin-trial milestone for the proposal reviewed at v150): refreshed the direct chromestatus listing (v151: Origin trial) and API (updated 2026-06-17 — OT desktop 151–154 registerable, ship unset, 'Proposed'), re-fetched the WICG spec (IDL unchanged, two static supports overloads), and verified Chromium main subtle_crypto.idl. Found and fixed the family index card's 'crypto.subtle.supports(...)' binding error, corrected the lede to distinguish five crypto.subtle instance methods from static SubtleCrypto.supports(), and made example feature detection safe even when the SubtleCrypto global is absent by starting from globalThis. The underlying runtime concept implementations were already binding-correct. Renamed 'why it shipped' to 'why it's proposed' with an exact availability statement, removed unsupported audited/default-TLS claims, made the probe flag-note precise that matrix cells read '—' (unknown) while the method is absent, and fixed the post-interaction single-probe overflow found at 390px. Added the immutable root suite (9 assertions incl. the static-vs-instance anti-regression pair). Missing-suite denominator 11→10 expected (788/798).
